words.story: The young scribe had to rewrite the ancient scroll. Rewrite each rune carefully. 'Rewrite until it's perfect,' said the master. They rewrote the spell twelve times. Rewriting the prophecy saved the kingdom. The magic quill could rewrite history. Rewrite the errors, rewrite the fate. After twelve rewrites, the text glowed. The goblin tried to rewrite the contract but failed. Rewrite gave them a second chance. Everyone learned to rewrite their mistakes.
words.cloze_context: The ink had faded, so the librarian had to ______ the ancient spell.

words.story: The wizard allowed a preview of the new spell. Preview the potion before drinking. 'A preview prevents surprises,' he said. They held a preview for the royal court. Twelve previews were scheduled that month. The crystal ball gave a preview of the future. Preview the magical map before the journey. The dragon demanded a preview of the treasure. After the preview, everyone felt prepared. Preview became a standard safety step.
words.cloze_context: The students were given a ______ of the final exam questions to reduce anxiety.

words.story: The vanished castle would reappear every full moon. Reappear from the mist like a dream. 'Watch it reappear,' whispered the guide. Twelve times the ghost would reappear that night. The lost key might reappear in your pocket. The fairy promised to reappear at dawn. Reappearing was harder than disappearing. The spell made the stars reappear after the eclipse. Each time the hero fell, he would reappear stronger. Reappear taught them about hope and cycles.
words.cloze_context: After vanishing for a century, the legendary sword is said to ______ when the kingdom needs it most.

words.story: The magic key could unlock any door. Unlock the chest, unlock the secret. 'Unlock your potential,' the wizard said. Twelve locks were unlocked in one minute. The spell needed a whisper to unlock. They tried to unlock the ancient vault. Unlocking the portal required three words. The goblin's heart finally unlocked with kindness. Unlock the cage, free the phoenix. After unlocking all barriers, they found the treasure.
words.cloze_context: The riddle's answer would ______ the hidden chamber beneath the oak tree.

words.story: It was easy to misunderstand the dragon's growl. Misunderstand the spell could cause chaos. 'Please don't misunderstand my warning,' said the elf. Twelve times they misunderstood the prophecy. A single word could change everything if you misunderstand. The goblin felt hurt when they misunderstood his gift. Misunderstanding led to the great quarrel. After clearing the misunderstanding, peace returned. They learned not to misunderstand the ancient runes. Misunderstand became a lesson in patience.
words.cloze_context: The wizard's cryptic note was easy to ______, leading to a week of confusion.

words.story: The dragon's furnace would overheat without water. Overheat the cauldron and the potion explodes. 'Don't overheat the crystals,' warned the alchemist. Twelve times they let the engine overheat. The cooling spell prevented overheat. Overheating melted the magic rings. They installed a charm to detect overheat. After overheat, the forge had to rest. Learning to avoid overheat saved their workshop.
words.cloze_context: If you cast the fireball too many times, your wand will ______ and might crack.

words.story: The library had a section for magical nonfiction. Nonfiction books contained true spells. 'Read nonfiction to learn facts,' said the owl. Twelve nonfiction volumes covered dragon biology. The young witch preferred nonfiction over fairy tales. Nonfiction helped her pass the exam. They wrote a nonfiction account of the great war. Nonfiction records never lie. The council required nonfiction sources for research.
words.cloze_context: Unlike fairy tales, the wizard's reference book was ______, full of real historical events.

words.story: The submarine could submerge to great depths. Submerge the magic pearl in moonlight. 'Don't submerge the fire stone,' warned the sage. Twelve times they watched the sun submerge into the sea. The spell required them to submerge completely. Submerging the amulet revealed its power. The kelpie taught them how to submerge safely. After submerging, they found the sunken city. Submerge became a ritual of renewal.
words.cloze_context: To activate the water rune, you must ______ it in the enchanted spring at midnight.

words.story: The ghost was almost transparent in daylight. Transparent glass allowed them to see the potion. 'Be transparent in your dealings,' said the queen. Twelve transparent crystals hung in the window. The dragon's scales turned transparent when invisible. A transparent shield protected the city. They created a transparent map of the underground. Transparency built trust among the clans. The wizard valued a transparent contract.
words.cloze_context: The liquid became perfectly ______, revealing the golden coin at the bottom.

words.story: The healer discovered a magical antibiotic. Antibiotic potions cured the plague. 'An antibiotic fights evil germs,' she explained. Twelve batches of antibiotic saved the village. The antibiotic was made from moonflower. Without antibiotic, the fever would return. They shared the antibiotic recipe with all kingdoms. The antibiotic worked even on dragonpox. Antibiotic research became the top priority. Thanks to antibiotic, no one suffered needlessly.
words.cloze_context: The old witch's brew acted like a natural ______, stopping the infection within hours.

words.story: The royal guard managed to intercept the dark message. Intercept the spy before he escapes. 'We must intercept the cursed scroll,' said the captain. Twelve attempts to intercept the delivery failed. The hawk was trained to intercept magical letters. Intercepting the goblin's plan saved the castle. A clever spell could intercept any arrow. After intercepting the threat, they celebrated. Intercept became a key defensive strategy.
words.cloze_context: The owl was ordered to ______ any suspicious raven carrying dark magic.

words.story: The wizards formed a semicircle around the cauldron. A semicircle of stones marked the ritual site. 'Sit in a semicircle for the spell,' instructed the elder. Twelve semicircles were drawn in the sand. The amphitheater was built as a semicircle. A semicircle of light protected them. The dragon curled into a semicircle to sleep. They arranged the crystals in a semicircle for maximum power. Semicircle shapes focused energy.
words.cloze_context: The students sat in a ______ around the teacher to watch the demonstration.

words.story: The postwar era brought reconstruction and hope. Postwar treaties were signed under a peace tree. 'Postwar life is precious,' said the old soldier. Twelve postwar festivals celebrated unity. They built monuments in the postwar years. Postwar magic focused on healing. The postwar generation never knew battle. Postwar trade flourished between enemies. They wrote a chronicle of postwar miracles. Postwar lessons taught them to value peace.
words.cloze_context: The enchanted garden was planted in the ______ period to symbolize rebirth.

words.story: The young hero had to overcome many trials. First, overcome the fear of darkness. Then overcome the maze of thorns. With courage, she could overcome any obstacle. The dragon's roar tried to overcome her will. She used kindness to overcome hatred. The magical shield helped overcome the curse. Each day she learned to overcome a little more. 'Overcome your doubts,' the elder advised. Together they could overcome the giant. The spell said: 'Overcome the impossible.' After twelve challenges, she finally managed to overcome the final boss.
words.cloze_context: The knight needed to ______ the dragon's fire with an enchanted shield.

words.story: The magic seeds would multiply overnight. Multiply the coins by saying the spell. 'To multiply is to grow abundance,' said the fairy. Twelve rabbits multiplied into a hundred. The sorcerer's staff could multiply any object. They tried to multiply the food supply. Multiplying gems made them lose value. The rule: never multiply living things. After multiplying roses, the garden overflowed. Multiplication magic required careful balance.
words.cloze_context: The enchanted bread would ______ every time you broke a piece, feeding the entire village.

words.story: The magic play had a short intermission. During intermission, they ate glowing snacks. 'Intermission is for reflection,' said the director. Twelve minutes of intermission felt too short. The orchestra played softly through intermission. Intermission allowed the actors to recharge. They used intermission to discuss the plot. The dragon demanded an extra intermission. After intermission, the second act began. Intermission became their favorite part.
words.cloze_context: Halfway through the wizard's performance, there was a fifteen‑minute ______ for snacks.

words.story: The elves communicated through nonverbal signs. Nonverbal magic didn't require spoken words. 'Nonverbal spells are more powerful,' claimed the sage. Twelve nonverbal cues helped them avoid danger. The dragon understood nonverbal gestures perfectly. A nod, a blink — all nonverbal. They developed a whole nonverbal language for stealth. Nonverbal teaching helped the mute wizard. Mastering nonverbal communication saved them in silence zones.
words.cloze_context: The forest spirits only respond to ______ signals, so the party had to learn their gestures.

words.story: The gnomes taught everyone to recycle magic dust. Recycle old potions into new ones. 'Recycle to save the environment,' they chanted. Twelve recycling bins were placed around town. They recycled broken wands into kindling. The dragon recycled its scales for armor. Recycle the glass bottles for fresh spells. After recycling, the forest became cleaner. Recycling became a daily habit. The kingdom won an award for recycling.
words.cloze_context: Instead of throwing away the expired charm, the wizard decided to ______ its ingredients.

words.story: The magic bag would unzip by itself. Unzip the portal to another dimension. 'Unzip carefully,' warned the goblin tailor. Twelve zippers were undone in a second. The spell could unzip any seam. Unzipping the sky revealed the stars. The tent would unzip from inside. After unzipping, they found hidden pockets. Unzip the scroll to read the prophecy. Learning to unzip safely was essential.
words.cloze_context: The enchanted suitcase would only ______ when you whispered the password.

words.story: The magic broom would misbehave without polish. Misbehave and you'll get detention. 'Don't misbehave in the wizard's class,' said the prefect. Twelve times the imp misbehaved at the banquet. Misbehaving runes caused the spell to fail. They had a rule: no misbehaving near the cauldron. The fairy misbehaved by turning the milk sour. After misbehaving, the culprit had to clean the dungeon. Misbehave less, learn more.
words.cloze_context: The enchanted quill tends to ______ and write silly poems instead of the dictation.

words.story: The magic well would overflow during the full moon. Overflow the cauldron and you'll flood the lab. 'Don't let the potion overflow,' warned the recipe. Twelve times the river overflowed its banks. The cup of plenty never overflowed. Overflowing joy filled the hall. They built a dam to prevent overflow. Overflow of magic dust created a rainbow. After overflow, they learned to measure carefully.
words.cloze_context: The fountain of youth can ______ if too many wishes are made at once.

words.story: The princess was bilingual in Elvish and Common. Bilingual students could read ancient scrolls. 'Being bilingual is a superpower,' said the tutor. They created a bilingual dictionary for magic words. Twelve bilingual spells were carved on the wall. The bilingual dragon spoke both fire and water languages. A bilingual fairy helped negotiate peace. Even the goblins became bilingual to trade. The school offered bilingual classes. Bilingual wizards were highly respected. The kingdom thrived because of bilingual citizens.
words.cloze_context: The young diplomat was ______, speaking both human and fairy languages fluently.

words.story: The wizard decided to postpone the exam due to the storm. 'We must postpone the festival,' announced the queen. Postponing the dragon race disappointed many. They had to postpone twelve events that month. A magical fog forced them to postpone the tournament. 'Don't postpone what you can do today,' said the wise elf. But sometimes postponing saved lives. They postponed the battle to negotiate peace. The spell allowed them to postpone time itself. After postponing the eclipse, they studied its magic. Finally, they postponed nothing anymore.
words.cloze_context: Due to the unexpected blizzard, the headmaster decided to ______ the final exam.

words.story: The magic tournament reached the semifinal round. The semifinal match was between wizards and elves. Everyone watched the semifinal with excitement. The semifinal spell required great skill. Twelve contestants entered the semifinal. The semifinal winner would face the dragon. 'The semifinal is your hardest test,' said the master. They trained day and night for the semifinal. The semifinal lasted three hours. In the end, a young witch won the semifinal. The crowd cheered for the semifinal champion.
words.cloze_context: After winning the quarterfinal, the team advanced to the ______ round of the magic cup.

words.story: The little gnome rode a magical tricycle. The tricycle had three glowing wheels. 'A tricycle is easier than a bicycle,' he said. He used the tricycle to deliver potions. Twelve tricycles raced through the village. The fairy's tricycle could fly. The goblin invented a tricycle that swam. Everyone loved the tricycle parade. The tricycle bell chimed like a wind chime. After the race, the tricycle was decorated with flowers. The tricycle became a symbol of childhood magic.
words.cloze_context: The young dwarf learned to ride a ______ before moving on to a two‑wheeled bike.

words.story: The wizard regretted his inaction during the crisis. Inaction allowed the darkness to grow. 'Inaction is still a choice,' said the queen. They suffered because of inaction. Twelve villages were lost due to inaction. The hero vowed to replace inaction with courage. Inaction of the council frustrated everyone. The spell against inaction was cast. Inaction froze the guards in place. Finally, they broke free from inaction and fought. Inaction was never an option again.
words.cloze_context: The kingdom fell not because of evil, but because of the council's ______.

words.story: The young mage studied magical geography. Geography of the enchanted lands was fascinating. They learned geography of the cloud kingdoms. 'Geography shapes destiny,' said the professor. Twelve geography tests later, they became experts. The atlas contained geography of all realms. Geography helped them navigate the underground. Even the dragons studied geography. Geography of the star islands was mapped. Without geography, they would be lost. The final exam was a geography quiz.
words.cloze_context: To find the hidden valley, you must study the ______ of the Whispering Woods.

words.story: The wizard school offered magical biology. Biology of phoenixes was their favorite. 'Biology explains why dragons breathe fire,' said the teacher. Twelve biology lessons covered unicorns, griffins, and trolls. The biology of plants could heal wounds. They performed biology experiments on glowing mushrooms. Biology helped them breed three‑headed dogs. The final biology project was to create a new creature. Biology merged science and magic. Without biology, they wouldn't understand life.
words.cloze_context: The class on ______ studied how magic affects living creatures in the enchanted forest.

words.story: The alchemist used a microscope to see magic dust. The microscope revealed tiny runes on fairy wings. 'A microscope can see the unseen,' he said. Twelve discoveries were made with the microscope. They found microscopic dragons using the microscope. The microscope helped study pixie sweat. Without a microscope, the cure would remain hidden. The enchanted microscope could see through dimensions. Every wizard wanted a microscope. The microscope became their most valuable tool.
words.cloze_context: The potion master used a powerful ______ to examine the tiny crystals in the elixir.

words.story: The archmage could teleport across continents. Teleport required immense concentration. They used a teleport circle to travel instantly. 'Teleport safely,' warned the scroll. Twelve teleport tests were performed. The young wizard learned to teleport without error. Teleport mishaps could land you in a tree. The teleport key was guarded by goblins. After mastering teleport, they visited all kingdoms. Teleport made the world small. They teleported home for dinner every night.
words.cloze_context: The emergency spell allows you to ______ to the safety of the castle in one second.

words.story: The merpeople built a magical aquarium. The aquarium housed water dragons and sea serpents. 'Our aquarium is the largest,' they boasted. Twelve aquariums were connected by tunnels. The children loved visiting the aquarium. The aquarium had a glass bottom boat. An enchanted aquarium floated in the sky. The aquarium's water never froze. They rescued injured sea creatures and brought them to the aquarium. The aquarium became a school. Everyone learned aquatic magic there.
words.cloze_context: The underwater palace has a giant ______ where rare bubble‑fish are kept.
